column4: Standardize backend API error responses with centralized ProblemDetail
column6: Add handlers or overrides for validation, binding, unreadable body, and fallback exceptions; preserve domain exception semantics; add HTTP tests for invalid body, invalid query/path param, forbidden, not found, conflict, and unexpected server error.
column7: Invalid request body returns consistent 400 shape; invalid query/path parameter returns same standard shape; forbidden/not found/conflict/unprocessable are consistent; unexpected server errors return sanitized 500 response.

column4: Reduce duplicated ProblemDetail construction across exception classes
column6: Extract shared ProblemDetail construction logic and refactor existing exception types to use it without changing public behavior.
column7: Existing exception types keep the same status and core fields; duplicate factory logic is materially reduced.

column4: Replace API-leaking IllegalArgumentException with typed domain/API exceptions
column6: Audit service methods where IllegalArgumentException can propagate to HTTP and replace user-facing cases with typed exceptions.
column7: Representative flows return intentional ProblemDetail responses; no user-facing business validation relies on raw IllegalArgumentException.

column4: Add HTTP contract tests for standardized backend error responses
column6: Add contract tests for validation, forbidden, not found, conflict, and unexpected server errors.
column7: Contract tests fail on response-shape drift and cover representative entry points.

column4: Introduce Spring MVC actor injection to remove direct RequestScopes access from controllers
column6: Implement ActorContext injection via Spring MVC and migrate a small pilot set of controllers.
column7: Pilot controllers no longer call RequestScopes or ActorContext.fromRequestScopes() directly; behavior remains unchanged.

column4: Refactor org settings endpoints into thin controller plus service validation flow
column6: Remove repeated actor extraction and move logo validation out of the controller while keeping the endpoint contract unchanged.
column7: Controller methods are limited to request parsing, service invocation, and response creation; file validation is not inline.

column4: Move portal authentication orchestration out of controller
column6: Create an application service for request-link and exchange flows; move tenant scoping and repository interactions out of the controller.
column7: Each endpoint delegates to a single application service call; controller no longer binds tenant context directly.

column4: Remove repository-backed response enrichment from selected controllers
column6: Refactor billing rate and comment endpoints to use reusable response assemblers or application services for enrichment.
column7: Controllers do not inject repositories for response enrichment; API payloads stay unchanged.

column4: Eliminate direct repository injection from non-dev controllers
column6: Audit non-dev controllers importing repositories and move repository usage into services or query layers.
column7: Non-dev controllers no longer inject repositories directly, or any exceptions are explicitly documented.

column4: Move large nested controller DTOs into dedicated request/response classes
column6: Externalize the largest embedded DTO groups while preserving JSON and validation behavior.
column7: Target controllers are materially shorter and behavior is unchanged.

column4: Replace string action parameters with enums and Spring validation
column6: Identify endpoints using action strings and replace manual string switching with enums and standard validation.
column7: Invalid action values return the standard validation error contract.

column4: Decompose invoice service into focused collaborators
column6: Split invoice creation, mutation, transitions, integrations, rendering, validation, events, and query concerns into coherent collaborators.
column7: Dependency count and class size are materially reduced while behavior is preserved.

column4: Decompose billing run service into lifecycle selection and generation workflows
column6: Separate billing run lifecycle, entry selection, invoice generation, and post-processing into smaller workflow-oriented services.
column7: Core workflows are isolated and the top-level service becomes orchestration-only or disappears.

column4: Identify and plan the next oversized services for decomposition
column6: Review remaining large services and produce a ranked queue with responsibility maps and extraction proposals.
column7: A prioritized shortlist exists for the next five large services.

column4: Move reusable business invariants out of controller or service branching
column6: Extract stable rules and transitions into domain methods, policy classes, or rule components.
column7: Representative domains have isolated rule logic with focused tests.

column4: Centralize reusable validation logic for files ranges and action inputs
column6: Identify repeated validation logic and consolidate it into shared validators integrated with the API error contract.
column7: At least three duplicated validation patterns are centralized.

column4: Introduce tenant-aware test fixtures and helpers for backend integration tests
column6: Add reusable helpers for tenant setup, actor context, and seeded entities for service and HTTP tests.
column7: At least three representative test classes migrate to the new fixtures and repeated setup ceremony is reduced.

column4: Define and document backend test taxonomy and coupling rules
column6: Write a concise backend testing guide that defines HTTP contract, service, repository, and cross-tenant/security test boundaries.
column7: A concise testing guideline exists and is usable for new tests.

column4: Reduce implementation coupling in selected backend integration tests
column6: Refactor selected tests to rely more on fixtures and observable behavior and less on repositories, transactions, and raw scope plumbing.
column7: Selected tests use less direct repository and scope plumbing while preserving coverage intent.

column4: Enforce backend architecture rules with automated tests
column6: Add architecture tests to enforce no repository injection in controllers, no raw scope access in controllers, and standard error-handling conventions.
column7: Build fails on core architectural rule violations with limited false positives.

column4: Add guardrails for oversized services and controllers
column6: Define and enforce or report thresholds for class size and constructor dependency count.
column7: Thresholds are documented and local or CI checks can flag new violations.

column4: Add backend contribution checklist for controller exception and test quality
column6: Document controller thinness, exception policy, and test coupling rules for future contributors.
column7: A concise checklist exists and is easy to reference in reviews.
